summary: Linked the Mr. Game & Watch parachute item unit by adding the missing ItemStateTable data definition, preserving/reordering callback bodies for object layout, and switching itgamewatchparachute.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The automated GALE01 report confirmed one new match for main/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute .data, +32 bytes from 0.00% to 100.00%, with linked code/data gains.
what_changed: Changed configure.py to build melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute.c as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Added ItemStateTable it_803F78F8[] with two entries, state IDs 0 and 1, both using itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim and NULL for the remaining callbacks.
Moved itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_Destroyed earlier in the file without changing its logic: it gets the Item via GET_ITEM and calls ftGw_AttackAirN_ItemParachuteSetFlag when ip->owner is non-NULL.
Moved itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_EvtUnk to the end of the file without changing its wrapper behavior around it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj).
Left the small item wrappers it_802C6DB8 and it_802C6DD8 as direct calls to it_8026B724 and it_8026B73C respectively.
smart_moves: Added the explicit ItemStateTable instead of relying on an undeclared/external data blob, which produced the reported +32 byte .data match.
Kept callback bodies semantically unchanged while moving them, minimizing gameplay risk and targeting object/source-order matching.
Only marked the unit Matching after the C/data layout was sufficient for the decomp.dev report to show the parachute unit .data at 100.00%.
decomp_lessons: For item units, matching can depend on declaring state tables in C with the exact entry count, order, callback pointers, and NULL slots.
Source order of functions can matter for matching/linking; moving identical callback definitions may be enough to align object layout without altering behavior.
A unit can already have mostly decompiled logic but remain NonMatching until associated .data, such as ItemStateTable entries, is represented correctly.
After a Game & Watch item unit matches, remember to update configure.py from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
naming_conventions: Data symbol kept its address-style name: it_803F78F8.
Unrenamed helper functions retain address-style names such as it_802C6C38, it_802C6D6C, it_802C6DB8, and it_802C6DD8.
Logic callback names use the item/action form itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_Destroyed, itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_PickedUp, and itGameWatchParachute_Logic74_EvtUnk.
The animation callback in the state table uses the existing spelling/casing itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim, which differs from the GameWatchParachute casing of the Logic74 names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Materialize the .data table as ItemStateTable it_803F78F8[] with two initializer rows rather than leaving the unit without its state-table data.
Use function definition ordering as a matching lever: Destroyed was moved above it_802C6D6C and EvtUnk was moved after itGamewatchparachute_UnkMotion1_Anim.
Preserve thin wrapper functions exactly when reordering: EvtUnk still calls it_8026B894, it_802C6DB8 still calls it_8026B724, and it_802C6DD8 still calls it_8026B73C.
Promote the object in configure.py only when the file is ready to link as Matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body rationale were present in this slice.

Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 reported linked code 38.83% (+0.02%, +776 bytes), matched data 38.81% (+32 bytes), linked data 33.14% (+40 bytes), and one new match for main/melee/it/items/itgamewatchparachute .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.
